mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates tightening labor markets, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain negative in 70%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in residential construction permits (HOUST) above 1.2 million units signals strong housing demand, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show negative correlation with HOUST above 1.2 million units (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 99.0 suggests weakening supply chain activity, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below 99.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 1.5% signal monetary tightening, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show positive correlation with combined CPIAUCSL > 2.5 and FEDFUNDS > 1.5 (Rank IC > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3.0% combined with rising residential sales (RSAFS) predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days due to strong economic momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns show negative correlation with combined GDPC1 > 3.0 and RSAFS > 465000 (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150,000 combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to strong labor market dynamics.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show negative correlation with combined PAYEMS > 150,000 and UNRATE < 4.5 (Rank IC < -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 2.0% combined with stable federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as deflationary risks recede.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show negative correlation with combined CPIAUCSL < 2.0 and FEDFUNDS < 1.0 (Rank IC < -0.05).
